curation.synopsis: Seventeen-year-old Arvin Larn is conscripted, drilled and shipped to war, and then, through a clerk's error, delivered to the wrong front entirely. He arrives at Broucheroc, a ruined hive-city that the under-strength Vardan Rifles have held against ceaseless Ork assault for over a decade. The grim arithmetic of the trenches gives a fresh replacement a life expectancy of fifteen hours. Across a single brutal day Larn endures snipers, friendly fire, indifferent officers, foul rations and an all-out greenskin offensive, learning what survival actually costs on a front the Imperium has all but forgotten. A ground-level portrait of the ordinary soldier in an endless war.
